record.field: Rybbit is an open-source, privacy-friendly web and product analytics platform built as an intuitive alternative to Google Analytics.

Rybbit is an open-source, privacy-friendly web and product analytics platform built as an intuitive alternative to Google Analytics.

It tracks visitors without cookies, fingerprinting, or storing personally identifiable information, which keeps deployments aligned with GDPR and CCPA while capturing data on every visitor instead of only those who accept a consent banner. Rybbit pairs a clean, real-time dashboard with advanced product-analytics capabilities, and is designed for teams that want full ownership of their data by self-hosting via Docker Compose. The self-hosted edition has feature parity with the cloud service, backed by ClickHouse for fast queries on large datasets.

Key features
  • Cookieless, privacy-first tracking with daily-rotating salted IDs and no PII storage
  • Core web metrics: sessions, unique users, pageviews, bounce rate and session duration
  • Funnels, user journeys, retention analysis, goals and custom events with JSON properties
  • Session replay, error tracking and Web Vitals performance monitoring
  • Three-level geolocation (country, region, city) with map visualizations and 15+ filter dimensions
  • Multi-site organizations, public dashboards and a lightweight script that integrates with 40+ platforms
Best for

Developers, startups and privacy-conscious teams that want a modern, self-hostable analytics stack with full data ownership and no consent-banner friction, as a drop-in replacement for Google Analytics, Plausible or similar tools.

services: rybbit_clickhouse: image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:25.5 volumes: - ./clickhouse_data:/var/lib/clickhouse - ../files/clickhouse_config:/etc/clickhouse-server/config.d environment: - CLICKHOUSE_DB={{ clickhouse_db }} - CLICKHOUSE_USER={{ clickhouse_user }} - CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD={{ clickhouse_password }} healthcheck: test: - CMD - wget - --no-verbose - --tries=1 - --spider - http://localhost:8123/ping interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 start_period: 10s restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_rybbit_clickhouse' networks: - internal rybbit_postgres: image: postgres:17.5 environment: - POSTGRES_DB={{ postgres_db }} - POSTGRES_USER={{ postgres_user }} - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{ postgres_password }} volumes: - ./postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U ${{ postgres_user }} -d ${{ postgres_db }} interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_rybbit_postgres' networks: - internal rybbit_backend: image: ghcr.io/rybbit-io/rybbit-backend:v1.5.1 environment: - NODE_ENV=production - CLICKHOUSE_HOST=http://rybbit_clickhouse:8123 - CLICKHOUSE_DB={{ clickhouse_db }} - CLICKHOUSE_USER={{ clickhouse_user }} - CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD={{ clickhouse_password }} - POSTGRES_HOST=rybbit_postgres - POSTGRES_PORT=5432 - POSTGRES_DB={{ postgres_db }} - POSTGRES_USER={{ postgres_user }} - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{ postgres_password }} - BETTER_AUTH_SECRET={{ better_auth_secret }} - BASE_URL={{ base_url }} - DOMAIN_NAME={{ domain_name }} - DISABLE_SIGNUP={{ disable_signup }} depends_on: rybbit_clickhouse: condition: service_healthy rybbit_postgres: condition: service_started healthcheck: test: - CMD - wget - --no-verbose - --tries=1 - --spider - http://127.0.0.1:3001/api/health interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 start_period: 10s restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3001' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network rybbit_client: image: ghcr.io/rybbit-io/rybbit-client:v1.5.1 environment: - NODE_ENV=production - NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL={{ base_url }} - DOMAIN_NAME={{ domain_name }} - NEXT_PUBLIC_DISABLE_SIGNUP={{ disable_signup }} depends_on: - rybbit_backend restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_rybbit_client' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T rybbit_postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d rybbit_postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T rybbit_postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T rybbit_postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T rybbit_postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0

<clickhouse> <logger> <level>warning</level> <console>true</console> </logger> <query_thread_log remove="remove"/> <query_log remove="remove"/> <text_log remove="remove"/> <trace_log remove="remove"/> <metric_log remove="remove"/> <asynchronous_metric_log remove="remove"/> <session_log remove="remove"/> <part_log remove="remove"/> <latency_log remove="remove"/> <processors_profile_log remove="remove"/> </clickhouse>
